scenarios.title: Calculate flat rate margin with higher short market value in sub-group
scenarios.intent: Verify flat rate margin calculation correctly includes positions with higher absolute market value and applies margin multiplier
given: A clearing participant has positions in multiple sub-margin groups
Sub-group 1 has total absolute long market value of 30,000,000 HKD and total absolute short market value of 60,000,000 HKD
Sub-group 2 has total absolute long market value of 750,000 HKD and total absolute short market value of 0 HKD
Sub-group 3 has total absolute long market value of 0 HKD and total absolute short market value of 300,000 HKD
Flat margin rates are 12% for sub-group 1, 30% for sub-group 2, 55% for sub-group 3
A flat rate margin multiplier of 2 is assigned
when: The flat rate margin calculation is performed following Steps 1-3
then: All short sub-group 1 positions are included in the calculation
All long sub-group 1 positions are excluded from the calculation
All long sub-group 2 positions are included in the calculation
All short sub-group 3 positions are included in the calculation
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier equals 15,180,000 HKD
assumptions: Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are as specified in the example
Position identification per §3.2.2 has been completed

evidence.quote: Flat rate margin is calculated according to the steps as follows
the total absolute short market value is higher than the total absolute long market value. Therefore, all short sub-group 1 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin with equal long and short market values in sub-group
scenarios.intent: Verify behavior when total absolute long and short market values are equal within a sub-margin group
given: A clearing participant has positions in a sub-margin group
Total absolute long market value equals total absolute short market value at 50,000,000 HKD each
when: The flat rate margin Step 1 comparison is performed
then: The system determines which positions to include based on the tie-breaking rule
The calculation proceeds to Step 2 with the determined position set
assumptions: The tie-breaking behavior when long and short market values are equal is not explicitly defined in the evidence
System documentation or configuration may define the tie-breaking rule

evidence.quote: compare the total absolute market value of long positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group
take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them

scenarios.title: Validate sub-margin group classification and market value inputs
scenarios.intent: Verify that position data is correctly classified into sub-margin groups and market values are properly aggregated
given: A clearing participant has multiple positions across different instruments
Each position has an InstrumentID, Quantity, and market value in HKD equivalent
when: Positions are identified and classified per §3.2.2 instructions
Positions are grouped into sub-margin groups as defined on HKEX website
then: Each position is assigned to the correct sub-margin group
Absolute market value of long positions is aggregated separately from short positions
Market values are converted to HKD equivalent where necessary
assumptions: Sub-margin group definitions are available from HKEX website
Position identification per §3.2.2 has been completed correctly

evidence.quote: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market value of short positions separately for each position identified as per instructions in §3.2.2
each sub-margin group of flat rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website

scenarios.title: Round up aggregated margin to nearest rounding parameter
scenarios.intent: Verify aggregated market-risk-component margin is correctly calculated and rounded up to the nearest rounding parameter
given: Portfolio margin is 10,000,000 HKD
Flat rate margin is 15,180,000 HKD
Liquidation risk add-on is 266,865 HKD
Structured product add-on is 550,000 HKD
Corporate action position margin is 2,500,000 HKD
Holiday add-on is 18,433,039 HKD
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: Step 1 aggregates all market risk components
Step 2 rounds up the aggregated margin to the nearest 10,000
then: Aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,929,904 HKD
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,930,000 HKD
assumptions: All market risk component values are provided as inputs
Rounding parameter is retrieved from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on = 46,929,904
Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000)
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin already at rounding increment boundary
scenarios.intent: Verify rounding behavior when aggregated margin is already an exact multiple of the rounding parameter
given: Aggregated market-risk-component margin is 46,930,000 HKD
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: Step 2 rounds up the aggregated margin to the nearest 10,000
then: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin remains 46,930,000 HKD
No change occurs as the value is already at the rounding boundary
assumptions: Rounding up an exact multiple returns the same value

evidence.quote: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000

scenarios.title: Validate all market risk components are present for aggregation
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required market risk components are present and valid before aggregation
given: A clearing participant has positions requiring margin calculation
Market risk components include Portfolio margin, Flat rate margin, Liquidation risk add-on, Structured product add-on, Corporate action position margin, and Holiday add-on
when: Step 1 calculates aggregated margin derived from market risk components
then: All six market risk components are validated as present
Each component has a valid numeric value
Aggregation proceeds only when all components are available
assumptions: Missing or invalid components should trigger a validation error
Zero values are valid for components that do not apply

evidence.quote: Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components
Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on

scenarios.title: Calculate favorable MTM and derive net margin
scenarios.intent: Verify favorable MTM calculation and its deduction from rounded aggregated margin to derive net margin
given: Market value of portfolio is -300,550,000 HKD
Contract value of portfolio is -287,850,000 HKD
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin is 46,930,000 HKD
when: Step 1 calculates favorable MTM using the formula
Step 2 deducts favorable MTM from rounded aggregated margin
then: Favorable MTM equals -12,700,000 HKD (negative indicates MTM requirement)
Favorable MTM is zero when result is negative
Net margin equals Maximum(0, 46,930,000 - 0) = 46,930,000 HKD
assumptions: Negative favorable MTM result indicates MTM requirement, not favorable MTM
MTM requirement absolute value is added after applying margin credit per §3.2.6.1

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added after applying margin credit
Net margin = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)

scenarios.title: Net margin at zero when favorable MTM equals aggregated margin
scenarios.intent: Verify net margin calculation when favorable MTM exactly equals rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin
given: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin is 50,000,000 HKD
Favorable MTM is 50,000,000 HKD
when: Step 2 calculates net margin using Maximum(0, Rounded aggregated margin – Favorable MTM)
then: Net margin equals Maximum(0, 50,000,000 - 50,000,000) = 0 HKD
assumptions: This tests the boundary condition where net margin reaches zero floor

evidence.quote: Net margin = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)

scenarios.title: Validate portfolio market value and contract value inputs
scenarios.intent: Verify that portfolio market value and contract value inputs are valid for favorable MTM calculation
given: A clearing participant has a portfolio with positions
Portfolio has aggregated HKD equivalent market value and contract value
when: Step 1 calculates favorable MTM using Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: Market valuePortfolio is validated as a numeric value
Contract valuePortfolio is validated as a numeric value
Both values are aggregated HKD equivalent values
Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive
assumptions: Multiple currencies in portfolio are converted per Appendix 4.6
Numbers are rounded off on position level

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive
The aggregated value of HKD equivalent contract value and HKD equivalent market value

scenarios.title: Apply margin credit to net margin calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify margin credit is correctly applied to derive net margin after credit
given: Net margin is 46,930,000 HKD
Margin credit of 5,000,000 HKD is granted to the clearing participant
when: Net margin after credit is calculated using Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)]
then: Net margin after credit equals Maximum[0, (46,930,000 – 5,000,000)] = 41,930,000 HKD
assumptions: Margin credit value is normally 5,000,000 HKD as stated in the evidence

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation
Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Net margin after credit at zero boundary
scenarios.intent: Verify net margin after credit calculation when net margin equals margin credit
given: Net margin is 5,000,000 HKD
Margin credit is 5,000,000 HKD
when: Net margin after credit is calculated using Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)]
then: Net margin after credit equals Maximum[0, (5,000,000 – 5,000,000)] = 0 HKD
assumptions: This tests the boundary where net margin exactly equals margin credit

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Validate margin credit value for clearing participant
scenarios.intent: Verify that margin credit is correctly assigned and applied for each clearing participant
given: A clearing participant is registered in the system
Margin credit is granted to each CP
when: Margin calculation applies the margin credit
then: Margin credit value is retrieved for the specific clearing participant
Margin credit is a valid non-negative numeric value
Margin credit is applied correctly in the net margin after credit formula
assumptions: Margin credit value may vary per clearing participant
The normally 5,000,000 value is a default or typical value

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation

feature: Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement from all components
scenarios.intent: Verify total MTM and margin requirement is correctly derived by summing net margin after credit and other risk components
given: Net margin after credit is 41,930,000 HKD
MTM requirement is 12,700,000 HKD
Position limit add-on is 487,332 HKD
Credit risk add-on is 12,000,000 HKD
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000 HKD
when: Total MTM and margin requirement is calculated by adding net margin after credit to other risk components
assumptions: All risk component values are provided as inputs from prior calculation steps
Credit risk add-on is applicable (note: will not be applicable upon VaR Platform launch)

evidence.quote: total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin after credit to other risk components
Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components

scenarios.title: Total margin requirement with zero other risk components
scenarios.intent: Verify total MTM and margin requirement calculation when all other risk components are zero
given: Net margin after credit is 50,000,000 HKD
MTM requirement is 0 HKD
Position limit add-on is 0 HKD
Credit risk add-on is 0 HKD
Ad-hoc add-on is 0 HKD
when: Total MTM and margin requirement is calculated
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals 50,000,000 + 0 + 0 + 0 + 0 = 50,000,000 HKD
Total equals net margin after credit when no other risk components apply
assumptions: This tests the boundary where only net margin after credit contributes to total
Zero values for other risk components are valid inputs

evidence.quote: Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on

scenarios.title: Validate all risk components are present for total calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required risk components are present and valid before calculating total MTM and margin requirement
given: A clearing participant has completed margin calculations per §3.2.5 and §3.2.6
Risk components include Net margin after credit, MTM requirement, Position limit add-on, Credit risk add-on, and Ad-hoc add-on
when: Total MTM and margin requirement is derived from results under §3.2.5 and §3.2.6
then: Net margin after credit is validated as present and numeric
MTM requirement is validated as present and numeric
Position limit add-on is validated as present and numeric
Credit risk add-on is validated as present and numeric
Ad-hoc add-on is validated as present and numeric
assumptions: Missing or invalid components should trigger a validation error
Zero values are valid for components that do not apply

evidence.quote: Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement from Results under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6
Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on
